page: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. The processor operates at a speed of 2.4 GHz, features a 320 MB L3 cache, and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and integer operations.

The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a memory allocation of 8.0 GB per physical core. Local storage is a key feature of this instance, which bundles 7500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity directly to the hardware profile. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No graphics processing units (GPUs) are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7i.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance across multiple vectors. It achieves top-tier ratings in Geekbench single-core execution and image processing, alongside strong performance in uncached large-block memory read (206.98 GB/sec) and write (115.53 GB/sec) bandwidth tests. Cryptographic execution, compression, and LLM prompt processing also register strong results. However, text generation for large 70B LLM models represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% tier.

With its high-capacity DDR5 memory and massive local NVMe SSD storage, this instance delivers high resource density for data-intensive applications. It is highly suited for hosting large-scale relational and non-relational databases, transactional systems, data warehousing, and localized data analytics pipelines that require rapid storage access and high I/O throughput.
description: The Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, 256 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a bundled 7500 GB NVMe SSD. Designed for high-throughput data environments, it delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier Geekbench single-core and image processing performance, alongside strong uncached memory read/write speeds and robust cryptographic execution. While it excels at prompt processing and compression, text generation for large 70B models represents a performance tradeoff. This hardware profile provides excellent resource density for storage-heavy applications, making it highly suitable for large databases, transactional systems, and data-intensive analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong performance for data-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7.5 TB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, massive local NVMe SSD capacity, and advanced Intel Xeon processing for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor
256 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
7500 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and image processing benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and data analytics
